setting.note: Dostoevsky's second great Petersburg novel, mapped from the terminus of the Warsaw railway through the salons and the merchant streets to the leafy summer resort of Pavlovsk, thirty kilometres south, where much of the second half unfolds. A companion to Crime and Punishment: the same city, a different fever.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Fyodor Dostoevsky, first published 1869; the author died in 1881.
rights.translation: English translation from Project Gutenberg (public domain); Eva Martin.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US and UK/EU.
rights.attribution: Fyodor Dostoevsky, The Idiot (1869). Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.note: The dull red of the stone staircase in Rogojin's gloomy house on the Gorohovaya, the doomed house that opens and closes the tragedy. A deep red that clears WCAG AA on paper.
